LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER VICTOR PEREZ' PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED. IN REVIEWING THE RECORD, THE TRIAL COURT EXERCISES ITS INDEPENDENT JUDGMENT, WHILE AFFORDING A STRONG PRESUMPTION OF CORRECTNESS CONCERNING THE ADMINISTRATIVE FINDINGS. (SEE LAKE V. REED (1997) 16 CAL.4TH 448, 456; FUKUDA V. CITY OF ANGELS (1999) 20 CAL.4TH 805, 816-817.) THE WEIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE SUPPORTS THE HEARING OFFICER'S FINDINGS THAT THE PEACE OFFICER HAD REASONABLE CAUSE TO STOP PETITIONER'S VEHICLE AND PROBABLE CAUSE TO MAKE A LAWFUL ARREST. FURTHER, THE WEIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE SUPPORTS THE HEARING OFFICER'S FINDING THAT PETITIONER'S BLOOD ALCOHOL LEVEL WAS AT OR ABOVE .08% WHILE PETITIONER WAS DRIVING HIS MOTOR VEHICLE. THE WEIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE ALSO SUPPORTS THE FINDING THAT MR. ZEHNDER'S TESTIMONY IS NOT CREDIBLE, AND DOES NOT REBUT THE PRESUMPTION OF VEHICLE CODE 23152. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Hrg On Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Jan-23-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JAMES DUNN, ESQ., 650.918.7384, FOR PETITIONER VICTOR PEREZ.
KAREN DONALD, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, 510.622.2202, FOR RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PETITIONER VICTOR PEREZ' petition for writ of mandate is denied. In reviewing the record, the trial court exercises its independent judgment, while affording a strong presumption of correctness concerning the administrative findings. (See Lake v. Reed (1997) 16 Cal.4th 448, 456; Fukuda v. City of Angels (1999) 20 Cal.4th 805, 816-817.) The weight of the evidence supports the hearing officer's findings that the peace officer had reasonable cause to stop Petitioner's vehicle and probable cause to make a lawful arrest. Further, the weight of the evidence supports the hearing officer's finding that Petitioner's blood alcohol level was at or above .08% while Petitioner was driving his motor vehicle. The weight of the evidence also supports the finding that Mr. Zehnder's testimony is not credible, and does not rebut the presumption of Vehicle Code 23152. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: MARLA MILLER, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976
